CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.   
The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the reason for updating the document . Column 5 should include the names of the author (s) 
doing the changes. Including the name in column 6 shows that the document has been validated by the 
TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation means th e 
document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

2.0 May 2025 all Full reprocessing at a daily and 1/8° 
resolution (against weekly and 1/4° 
before). New correlation radii 
computed at each depth from T/S 
historical profiles. Review of the in 
situ observations errors to limit 
overfitting. Updated upstreams: SSS 
at 1/8° and SLA L4 DUACS24 1/8°; 
New MDT version, longitudes from -
180 to 180° instead of 0 to 360°. 
New static dataset with 
temperature and salinity monthly 
uncertainties  
N. Verbrugge, 
E. Greiner, P. 
Zunino 
H. Etienne

ACRONYM TABLE 
Acronym Description 
Argo International profiling float network (www.argo.net) 
CTD Conductivity, Temperature, and Depth; It refers to a package of 
electronic instruments that measure these properties 
DT Delayed Time 
EAN Estimated Accuracy Number 
EOF Extended Orthogonal Function 
GMSLA Global Mean Sea Level Anomaly 
MDT Mean Dynamic Topography 
MLD Mixed Layer Depth 
MY Multi-year 
NRT Near Real Time 
NRT Near Real Time 
REP Reprocessing 
RMS Root mean square 
SLA Sea Level Anomaly 
SSS Sea Surface Salinity 
SST Sea Surface Temperature 
STD Standard deviation 
TAC Copernicus Marine Service Thematic Assembly Centre 
XBT Expendable Bathythermograph

KEY DEFINITIONS 
RMSD (Root mean square 
differences) 𝑅𝑀𝑆𝐷 = √1
𝑁 ∑ (𝑋𝑝𝑖 − 𝑋𝑜𝑖 )
2𝑛
𝑖=1    ; N=samples number; Xp=product field 
value; Xo=observation value  
This metric measures the average magnitude of the difference of the 
product in comparison with a reference observed dataset. It is used in 
the validation of the product 
Quantiles In statistics, quantiles are points that divide an ordered set of data 
equally. A quantile indicates the value below which a percentage of the 
data lies. 
For example, if the value of the quantile 67% is 0.5, this means that 67% 
of the data in the sample is below 0.5 and the rest of the data is above 
0.5. 
STD 𝑆𝑇𝐷 = √1
𝑁 ∑ (𝐷𝑖 − 𝐷̅)2𝑛
𝑖=1  ; with D = Xp – Xo and Xp=product field value; 
Xo=observation value; N=samples number  
This metric measures the dispersion of the errors around the mean error. 
It is used in the validation of the product 
Climatology In the context of this document, a climatology is a static field that 
represents the average status of a variable over several years.

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes a multi-observation product for ocean physics, including temperature, salinity, geopotential heights, geostrophic currents, and mixed layer depth in near real-time and reprocessed datasets.

product_line.name: Multi Observation Global Ocean 3D Temperature Salinity Heights Geostrophic Currents and MLD Product
